description: Test corpus for the cors-security skill. CWE-942 lives in header/config values no deterministic gate scanner judges, so this is a schema-driven corpus: the runner smoke-validates fixture shape, and the detect/ignore pairs double as ground truth for the LLM eval harness. detect = a wildcard or unconditionally-reflected origin; ignore = the config-driven allowlist the skill mandates.

fixtures.reason: wildcard origin with credentials exposes the API to every site (CWE-942)

fixtures.text: const ALLOWED = new Set(['https://app.example.com']); app.use((req, res, next) => { const origin = req.headers.origin; if (ALLOWED.has(origin)) { res.setHeader('Access-Control-Allow-Origin', origin); res.setHeader('Access-Control-Allow-Credentials', 'true'); res.setHeader('Vary', 'Origin'); } next(); });
fixtures.reason: single specific origin echoed only when it matches the allowlist
unconditionally reflecting Origin is equivalent to a wildcard with credentials

fixtures.text: const ALLOWED = new Set(['https://app.example.com']); app.use((req, res, next) => { const origin = req.headers.origin; if (ALLOWED.has(origin)) { res.setHeader('Access-Control-Allow-Origin', origin); res.setHeader('Vary', 'Origin'); } next(); });
fixtures.reason: origin reflected only after an allowlist check

fixtures.text: const cors = require('cors'); app.use(cors({ origin: true, credentials: true }));
fixtures.reason: cors({ origin: true }) reflects any origin

fixtures.text: const cors = require('cors'); const ALLOWED = ['https://app.example.com']; app.use(cors({ origin: ALLOWED, credentials: true }));
fixtures.reason: explicit origin allowlist passed to the cors middleware
